title: Tithymalus collinus
taxonomy: Tithymalus collinus is a species within the Euphorbiaceae family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. The Euphorbiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the spurge family. Tithymalus collinus is further classified within the genus Tithymalus, which is a group of small to medium-sized succulent plants. The classification of Tithymalus collinus reflects its evolutionary history and its position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Tithymalus collinus, also known as Euphorbia collina, is a perennial plant species within the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its unique, succulent leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as diarrhea and ulcers, thanks to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Tithymalus collinus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tithymalus collinus is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
